tasks.title: Tool result contract and path-metadata normalization
tasks.definition_of_done: ToolResult (crates/opi-agent/src/tool.rs) gains a typed `truncated: bool` field and a typed optional `diagnostics` carrier; shared ToolResult success/error builders replace the ad-hoc serde_json::json! details blocks across all built-in tool branches, including bash timeout/cancel paths rather than only one success branch per tool. Path-addressed tools emit a uniform path-metadata block {workspace_root, path (user-facing), resolved_path, workspace_relation in {inside, outside, unresolved}} when a concrete path applies; command/search tools emit operation-specific metadata through the same builders instead of fake path fields. Bash operation metadata keeps a stable key set across success, timeout, cancellation, and nonzero-exit results: command, cwd, workspace_root, shell, exit_code (nullable when no process exit exists), timed_out, cancelled, truncated, and full_output reference when applicable. ToolResultMessage (crates/opi-ai/src/message.rs) carries truncated. grep/find/glob/ls details gain workspace_relation parity where they resolve a scoped/root path. Parametric tests iterate over each built-in tool success and representative failure/cancel result asserting the shared base contract, top-level truncated flag, and applicable path-or-operation metadata; a guard asserts no built-in tool constructs ToolResult with a hand-written details literal outside the builder.

tasks.title: Filesystem error taxonomy and per-cause diagnostic codes
tasks.definition_of_done: A shared filesystem/tool-error taxonomy enum (variants NotFound, NotAFile, NotADirectory, PermissionDenied, BinaryFile, UnsupportedEncoding, OutsideWorkspace, UnresolvedWorkspaceRoot) maps to distinct CODE_TOOL_* diagnostic codes in crates/opi-agent/src/diagnostic.rs, replacing the single CODE_TOOL_EXECUTION_FAILED collapse for tool-reported causes. resolve_tool_path reports symlink traversal (details.symlink_traversed or equivalent), workspace_relation, and unresolved roots consistently. Non-UTF-8 paths on platforms that expose them produce a clear UnsupportedEncoding diagnostic instead of silent U+FFFD replacement; valid Unicode paths round-trip in user-facing metadata. Windows drive/verbatim-prefix handling is covered by #[cfg(windows)] tests (skipped on Unix). read/ls/find path validation distinguishes NotFound vs NotAFile/NotADirectory vs PermissionDenied in messages and codes; file-content binary/encoding behavior is exercised by 11.3.

inference_notes.reason: Derived from design '## Filesystem Tool Policy' bullets (distinguish not found/not a file/not a directory/permission denied/binary file/unsupported encoding; avoid following symlinks without reporting it; Windows drive prefixes; non-UTF-8) and SC1/SC5.
Reuses the unified details/path-metadata builder and typed diagnostics carrier from 11.1.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: Missing paths, file-vs-directory mismatches, permission-denied paths, outside-workspace policy failures, and unresolved workspace roots each produce distinct CODE_TOOL_* diagnostics carried on the result and mirrored into trace. Symlink traversal through resolve_tool_path is reported instead of silently collapsed to outside-workspace.

On Windows resolve_tool_path handles C:foo, C:\\foo, and verbatim \\\\?\\C:\\foo prefixes deterministically without leaking the verbatim prefix into the user-facing path string; gated #[cfg(windows)] tests skip on Unix.
Interactive ReadTool remains AllowOutsideWorkspace (harness.rs run-mode selection); restriction stays in tool selection / mutating opt-in / extension hooks. Guard test pins it.

Valid Unicode filenames and directories round-trip through path resolution and result metadata without lossy replacement; invalid/non-UTF-8 paths use UnsupportedEncoding only on platforms that expose such paths.
